title: Methods, apparatus, systems, computer programs for enabling consumption of virtual content for mediated reality
abstract: A method comprising: in response to a determination that a user is not using a head-mounted apparatus, rendering to the user not using the head-mounted apparatus, a real-time audio notification in response to real-time virtual content consumable by the user when using the head-mounted apparatus in a real space, wherein the real-time audio notification directs a user to adopt a particular orientation in the real space for starting consumption, when using the head-mounted apparatus, of the real-time virtual content.
claims:
Claims 1 . A method comprising: in response to a determination that a user is not using a head-mounted apparatus, rendering to the user not using the head-mounted apparatus, a real-time audio notification in response to real-time virtual content consumable by the user when using the head-mounted apparatus in a real space, wherein the real-time audio notification directs a user to adopt a particular orientation in the real space for starting consumption, when using the head-mounted apparatus, of the real-time virtual content. 2. A method as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the head-mounted apparatus is a head- mounted display device and wherein the virtual content is virtual visual content, wherein in response to a determination that a user is not using the head-mounted display device, rendering to the user not using the head-mounted display device, the real-time audio notification in response to real-time virtual visual content consumable by the user when using the head-mounted display device in the real space, wherein the real-time audio notification directs the user to adopt a particular orientation in the real space for starting consumption, when using the head-mounted display device, of the real-time virtual visual content. 3. A method as claimed in any preceding claim comprising determining that the user is not using the head-mounted apparatus by monitoring over time one or more of orientation of the head-mounted apparatus, location of the head-mounted apparatus, proximity detection of a user's head at the head-mounted apparatus, location of a user. 4. A method as claimed in any preceding claim comprising determining that a user is not using a head-mounted apparatus by detecting one or more of: i) that the user has removed the head-mounted apparatus from the user's head; ii) that the head-mounted apparatus has moved outside a defined portion of the real space; iii) that the user has moved outside the defined portion of the real space. 5. A method as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the particular orientation is variable and depends upon the virtual content. 6. A method as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the real-time audio notification is a spatial notification generated at the particular orientation as a sound object having a particular location, in real space, that depends upon a mapping between the real space and 

 the virtual space and a virtual orientation or virtual orientation and location of the consumed virtual content in the virtual space. 7. A method as claimed in claim 6, wherein the sound object is generated in a head- mounted audio device worn by the user. 8. A method as claimed in any one of claims 6 or 7, wherein the sound object is generated by broadcasting audio from spatially distributed loudspeakers. 9. A method as claimed in claim 8, wherein the spatially distributed loudspeakers are provided by the head-mounted apparatus. 10. A method as claimed in claim 8 or claim 9, further comprising a calibration process to enable the spatially distributed loudspeakers to generate, for the user, the sound object at the particular location in the real space. 1 1 . A method as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising rendering the real-time audio notification in response to an event that occurs within the real-time virtual content consumable by the user wherein the particular orientation corresponds to the event. 12. A method as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the virtual content represents a virtual space at a virtual orientation of a notional viewer and that part of the virtual space at the virtual orientation of the notional viewer represented by the virtual content depends upon a mapping between the real space and the virtual space and the real orientation of the head- mounted apparatus in the real space. 13. A method as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein the real-time audio notification directs a user to adopt the particular orientation and, in addition, a particular location in the real space for starting consumption, when using the head-mounted display of the real-time virtual content. 14. An apparatus comprising means for performing the method of any preceding claim. 15. A computer program that when run by a processor enables the performance of any one of claims 1 to 13.
